title: Tracing, Evals, and Failure Analysis · Spiral Review
summary: Evaluate a multi-turn tool agent across realistic environments. Learners retrieve rules and procedures before seeing feedback. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for an agent trace and evaluation suite after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Record decisions, tool calls, results, state changes, timing, and identifiers without exposing private reasoning.
Evaluate action trajectories and state outcomes as well as final text.
Build deterministic environments and representative multi-turn cases.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""Bounded agent-loop portfolio. No external effects occur in this browser lab."""

from dataclasses import dataclass

steps.title: Separate evidence from confidence
steps.instruction:
Use a changed review case derived from this module: loops observability evals. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result.

Inspect the Regression Analysis trace for this review case. Which record connects a precondition, observed state change, and boundary result?
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for compare agent versions by material outcome slices and investigate regressions.

content.text: For Regression Analysis, distinguish a claim from evidence by locating the relevant precondition, trace or invariant, and changed input result.

hints: Name the Regression Analysis precondition or invariant that the review case must preserve.
Trace this concrete case before choosing: Use a changed review case derived from this module: loops observability evals. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result.
Use the documented misconception as a counterexample target: One average score safely determines release readiness.

stimulus.title: Regression Analysis case record
stimulus.caption: Three excerpts from a learner evidence packet; only one connects a claim to a reproducible check.

lines.label: assumption
lines.text: Use a changed review case derived from this module: loops observability evals. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result.

lines.label: risk
lines.text: One average score safely determines release readiness.

lines.label: evidence
lines.text: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Compare agent versions by material outcome slices and investigate regressions. Artifact under review: an agent trace and evaluation suite.

options.text: Apply the visible technique immediately, omit assumptions, and treat one successful output as complete proof.
The record names the assumption, applies Regression Analysis, and reports a changed-case check tied to the stakeholder decision.
The record says the result looks right but supplies no quantity, trace, condition, or counterexample.

options.text: Define the stakeholder decision, quantities, authority, and constraints
Choose and document the smallest defensible model or procedure
Execute the model while preserving inspectable intermediate evidence
Test boundaries and changed cases, communicate limits, and revise

steps.title: Record what required effort
steps.instruction: Write a concise evidence note that explains when Regression Analysis applies, what can fail, and how the changed case was verified.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Compare agent versions by material outcome slices and investigate regressions.
